high_reasoning_bridge.command_shape: timeout 180 hermes chat -Q --provider copilot -m gpt-5.5 --source tool -t safe -q '<strict-json-prompt>'

source_cards.do_not_publish_reason: Privacy/publication status requires review before any use.
source_cards.main_thesis: A LinkedIn search snippet shows David Aw posting about prompt engineering and asking questions, with context and loop engineer appearing in the result.
source_cards.safe_summary: LinkedIn search result for David Aw references a prompt-engineering post about how to ask, with context and loop engineer terms present.
source_cards.title: Feed post David Aw • 3rd+ A one man agile team | CISSP | 13x AWS Certs | Golden Jacket 41m • Follow Prompt engineering, how to ask. Context
useful_observations: The snippet links prompt engineering, context, and a loop engineer search term, but provides too little detail for evidentiary use.

source_cards.do_not_publish_reason: Privacy/publication status requires review before any use.
source_cards.main_thesis: A LinkedIn search result identifies Ameet C. with a technology and product-development profile, followed by a truncated post snippet mentioning a question.
source_cards.safe_summary: LinkedIn search result for Ameet C. describes technology and product-development work and includes a truncated post snippet plus loop engineer search term.
source_cards.title: Feed post Ameet C. • 3rd+ Driving innovation in technology and product development strategies. 1h • Follow I’m starting to wonder… how heavi
useful_observations: The result is a short LinkedIn snippet with insufficient context; it should not support a substantive claim.

candidate_claims: The snippet raises a question about whether monthly AI subscriptions may be subsidized, connected to token usage concerns.
source_cards.do_not_publish_reason: Privacy/publication status requires review before any use.
source_cards.main_thesis: A truncated LinkedIn search snippet questions AI subscription economics, suggesting possible subsidy concerns tied to heavy token consumption.
source_cards.safe_summary: Truncated LinkedIn snippet questions whether AI subscriptions are subsidized, apparently in relation to high token use and loop engineer search context.
source_cards.title: I’m starting to wonder… how heavily subsidized are the AI monthly subscriptions… especially since hashtag #tokenmaxxing Based on some math w
useful_observations: The snippet may flag an AI-cost discussion, but it is incomplete, private-adjacent, and unsuitable as evidence without review.

llm_note: Hermes CLI high-reasoning bridge requested; outputs must parse as strict JSON and schema-validate.

likely_files_to_change: possibly a new semantic-extraction report-only script after review
next_run_recommendation.rationale: source_notes can hold draft JSON without schema migration, but no-LLM cards are low-confidence and should be reviewed before semantic extraction.
next_run_recommendation.recommendation: Review persisted source-card drafts, then proceed to semantic extraction from persisted cards only if card quality and safety are acceptable; otherwise refine source-card heuristics/prompt first.

risks: No-LLM source cards are structural and low-confidence.
Real LLM prompts may produce unstable JSON unless schema-validated.
Sanitized source text may still include too much wording if max-summary limits are raised.
source_notes is generic and may be too limited for long-term source-card lifecycle/versioning.
A dedicated source_cards table may still be needed after persistence review.
Social/private-adjacent sources must remain discovery-only or needs-review until corroborated.
Vector DB chunks are not source authority and are not used by this script.

schema_assessment.assessment: The generated card object can be serialized as JSON in source_notes.note with note_type='source_card_draft' in a later run.
schema_assessment.recommendation: Use report-only output in Run 2; consider source_notes persistence in Run 3 after review.

schema_assessment.assessment: No new source columns are needed yet. Existing quality/privacy/duplicate fields are enough for draft generation.
schema_assessment.recommendation: Defer source table columns until real lifecycle states are proven necessary.

schema_assessment.assessment: A dedicated table could help later with versioning, model metadata, editor approval, and card hashes, but Run 2 does not prove it is required.
schema_assessment.recommendation: Defer dedicated table until at least one reviewed source_notes persistence run.

schema_assessment.assessment: Source cards look like a useful substrate, but semantic extraction and narrative packets remain higher-risk downstream steps.
schema_assessment.recommendation: Do not implement these in Run 2; revisit after source-card persistence safety is reviewed.
